subject: ela
title: Finding the Main Idea
content:
Finding the Main Idea

What is the Main Idea?

The main idea tells us what the whole text is about. It's the most important message the author wants to share. Everything else in the passage supports this big idea!

How to Find the Main Idea

1. Ask yourself: What is this passage mostly about?
2. Look at the title — it often hints at the topic
3. Find the key sentence that sums up the whole passage
4. Consider what detail all the other sentences support

Main Idea vs. Topic

• Topic = the subject (broader): Dogs
• Main Idea = the message about the topic (specific): Dogs make great family pets because they're loyal and easy to train.

Where to Find It

• Sometimes it's stated directly in one sentence (usually the first or last)
• Sometimes you have to figure it out by combining all the ideas

Practice Passage:

"Every year, millions of families go to the beach. The ocean is home to billions of creatures. Sand dollars, starfish, and crabs all live in the warm, shallow waters. The beach is also a popular place for people to swim and build sandcastles."

Main Idea: The beach is home to many different creatures and activities.

Finding Implied Main Idea

Sometimes the main idea is NOT stated directly. You need to infer it:
• Think about the title
• Consider the most repeated idea
• Ask: What point is the author making?

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of a passage about a dog named Max who learns new tricks, loves his owner, and goes to the park every day?
choices: Max is a happy dog who loves his life
questions.hint: What do all the details support?

questions.prompt: Where is the main idea usually stated in an informational text?
questions.hint: Look for the sentence that summarizes everything

questions.prompt: What is the difference between topic and main idea?
choices: Topic is what it's about; main idea is the message about it
Main idea is always in the title
questions.hint: Topic is the subject; main idea is the specific message

questions.prompt: A passage describes lions as strong, fast, and powerful predators. What is the main idea?
choices: Lions are the king of beasts
Lions are strong, fast, and powerful predators
questions.hint: Combine the details into one sentence

questions.prompt: If the main idea is not directly stated, what should you do?
choices: Look for clues and think about the title
questions.hint: Use clues like the title and key details
